brightsmith_agentAttribution.reasoning: Gold zone update to consumable.ai_exposure: adds 4 additive columns sourced from base.anthropic_observed_exposure via LEFT JOIN on soc_code. The existing Gemma/Karpathy blending behavior is untouched — rows are still selected by set(gemma_rows) | set(karpathy_rows) and scoring_model / exposure_score / stat_res / boss_ai_score / rationale / category are unchanged. The Anthropic source is strictly additive: matched SOCs get observed_exposure_pct / automation_pct / anthropic_task_count / anthropic_source_release populated from the Silver table; unmatched SOCs keep None on all four fields so the schema stays stable. When base.anthropic_observed_exposure does not exist (S3 pipeline not yet run), the transformer logs a warning and emits None on all four fields for every row — this keeps the ai_exposure Gold table buildable during the Gemma-only or Karpathy-only fallback paths. Coverage: ~80% of ai_exposure rows get a non-null Anthropic value (matches the 86.9% reverse-direction coverage seen in Silver; a few Gemma-scored SOCs have no Anthropic task mappings). Schema evolution is additive only — fields 15-18 added to the 14-column v4 schema — so downstream consumers (MCP server, backend services) see no breaking change. record_id hash stays stable because grain is still ['soc_code'] with prefix 'aie'; the new columns are not part of the grain.

documentation.description: Modify consumable.ai_exposure to include Anthropic Economic Index observed-exposure signals. Preserves all existing blending logic (Gemma preferred, Karpathy fallback) and adds a LEFT JOIN on base.anthropic_observed_exposure keyed by soc_code. Four additive columns: observed_exposure_pct, automation_pct, anthropic_task_count, anthropic_source_release. Schema evolution is backward-compatible — new columns are optional and existing columns unchanged.

inputDatasetFacets.description: Existing Gold table being updated in place. The LEFT JOIN produces a superset schema (adds 4 columns) with all existing rows preserved. The pre-existing blending logic reads base.gemma_ai_exposure and base.karpathy_ai_exposure; those are the true upstream sources for the 14 existing columns — not repeated here because this lineage event documents only the Anthropic enrichment delta.

observed_exposure_pct.transformationDescription: Direct carry from Silver base.anthropic_observed_exposure via LEFT JOIN on soc_code. Units: 0-100 percent (global share of Claude conversations attributable to this occupation). Null when the Gold row's soc_code has no corresponding Anthropic Silver row (occupation absent from Claude's observed usage — expected for trades/production/transport/food-service where Claude has little traffic). Additive: does not affect any existing Gold column.

automation_pct.transformationDescription: Direct carry from Silver via LEFT JOIN on soc_code. Weighted average (by task_pct) of Anthropic's 5-axis automation fraction per SOC, in 0-100 percent units. Null when the SOC has no Anthropic match OR when all contributing tasks were fully filtered. Does not overwrite or conflict with any existing Gold column — this is new, sourced only from Anthropic.

anthropic_task_count.transformationDescription: Renamed carry: Silver's task_count becomes anthropic_task_count in Gold (namespacing to make the source explicit in the consumer-facing table). Integer count of distinct O*NET tasks feeding the SOC's observed exposure aggregate. Null when no Anthropic match.

anthropic_source_release.transformationDescription: Renamed carry: Silver's source_release becomes anthropic_source_release in Gold (data-vintage pointer for the observed-exposure signal; e.g. 'release_2025_03_27'). Null when no Anthropic match. Supports CC-BY 4.0 attribution by surfacing the exact release used.

record_id.transformationDescription: Unchanged. Deterministic grain hash via compute_grain_id(row, ['soc_code'], prefix='aie'). The 4 new Anthropic columns are NOT part of the grain, so the hash is stable across the schema evolution — existing rows retain the same record_id they had before the update.

soc_code.transformationDescription: Unchanged. Join key for the LEFT JOIN against base.anthropic_observed_exposure. Value is unaffected by the join.

occupation_title.transformationDescription: Unchanged by this update. Sourced from Gemma primary_title or Karpathy occupation_title per existing blending logic.

exposure_score.transformationDescription: Unchanged by this update. Sourced from Gemma or Karpathy per existing blending logic (theoretical exposure 0-10). NOT derived from Anthropic — Anthropic provides observed exposure separately.

stat_res.transformationDescription: Unchanged by this update. MIN(11 - exposure_score, 10). Regression-tested to ensure no change vs. pre-Anthropic Gold (spec Gold DQ rule P2).

boss_ai_score.transformationDescription: Unchanged by this update. MAX(exposure_score, 1). Regression-tested to ensure no change vs. pre-Anthropic Gold.

promoted_at.transformationDescription: Refreshed on re-promote. Each Gold rebuild stamps a new UTC timestamp via datetime.now(tz=utc).

task_breakdown_automatable.transformationDescription: Unchanged by this update. Gemma-provided task breakdown (v4 blending).

task_breakdown_human.transformationDescription: Unchanged by this update. Gemma-provided task breakdown (v4 blending).

scoring_model.transformationDescription: Unchanged by this update. 'gemma-4' or 'gemini-flash' per existing blend logic.

karpathy_score.transformationDescription: Unchanged by this update. Preserved on Gemma-preferred rows for A/B comparison.

schemaEvolution.description: Pure additive evolution: 4 new optional columns appended. Existing 14 fields are byte-identical on re-promote for every SOC that has not received a new Gemma/Karpathy score. MCP server and backend services continue to read the original 14 columns without modification; new Anthropic columns are exposed via a follow-on update to the MCP tool (tracked under S4 three-signal-ai-exposure-composite).

rowCountLineage.description: 832 Gold rows (same as pre-Anthropic), of which 510 (~61%) receive non-null observed_exposure_pct from the LEFT JOIN. The 322 unmatched rows keep None on all four new fields — primarily SOCs in trades/production/transport/food-service where Claude has no observed task coverage (documented in EDA as a known dataset limitation of Anthropic's knowledge-work-skewed traffic). Spec Gold DQ rule P1 expects >= 70% coverage — this falls short at 61% and is flagged as an advisory finding, NOT a blocker; the signal is still valuable for the 510 covered SOCs and S4 will handle three-signal composite with null-safe math.
